info.title: BID — Batch Image Delivery API
info.description:
REST API for the **BID** (*Batch Image Delivery*) image-processing pipeline.

BID automates batch photo processing: automatic scaling, watermark overlay, EXIF cleaning, colour-space conversion, and delivery-folder export.  This API exposes the processing core as an OpenAPI 3.0-compliant service, decoupled from the legacy Tkinter UI.

## Road-map
| Phase | Milestone | Status |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Backend API Extraction | **Done** |
| 2 | WebSocket real-time layer | **Done** |
| 3 | React/TypeScript frontend | **In progress** |
| 4 | Event system | Planned |

## Auth
Protected endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.  Obtain tokens via `POST /api/v1/auth/login`.  Admin-only endpoints additionally require the `admin` role.

## Errors
All error responses use the `ErrorResponse` schema: `{ "detail": "...", "field": "..." }`.

get.description: Return HTTP 200 with body ``{"status": "ok"}`` when the service is alive.
Return the current API and BID product version strings.

Return aggregate queue metrics across all projects:

- **queue_length** — tasks waiting to start.
- **active_workers** — tasks currently executing.
- **max_workers** — concurrency ceiling (configured via `MAX_CONCURRENT_TASKS`).
- **completed_total** / **failed_total** — counters since last process restart.
- **utilization_pct** — `active_workers / max_workers × 100`.

Suitable for monitoring dashboards and alerting rules.

post.description:
Authenticate with *username* and *password*.

On success returns an **access token** (short-lived) and a **refresh token**
(long-lived).  Pass the access token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on
protected endpoints.

Exchange a valid *refresh_token* for a new access + refresh token pair.

Previously issued refresh tokens remain valid until they expire according to
the server's token lifetime settings; clients should discard old refresh
tokens once a new pair is obtained.

# TODO(security): implement refresh-token rotation — store JTIs in a DB
# revocation table and invalidate the previous token on re-issue.
# See: https://github.com/bajdero/BID/pull/26#discussion (C4)

get.description: Return all user records ordered by creation date. Requires admin role.

schema.title: Response List Users Api V1 Users Get

409.description: Username or email already in use.

post.description:
Create a new user account.  Returns **409 Conflict** if *username* or
*email* is already taken.

409.description: Username or email already in use.

put.summary: Update user role / state / email (admin only)

409.description: Username or email already in use.
Username or email already in use.

post.description:
Validate and enqueue the photos listed in *body.photos*.
Returns immediately; processing runs in the background.

400.description: Bad request — path traversal or invalid input.

post.summary: Enqueue all NEW photos in the project
post.description:
Scan the project source_folder and enqueue every photo whose state is
``new``, ``export_fail``, or ``error``.

400.description: Bad request — path traversal or invalid input.

delete.summary: Reset a photo to NEW state (re-queue)
delete.description:
Reset the photo identified by *folder/photo* back to state ``new`` so it
will be picked up by the next ``process/all`` run.

Returns HTTP 404 if the photo is not found in the source index.

schema.title: Response Reset Photo Api V1 Projects  Project Id  Process  Folder   Photo  Delete

400.description: Bad request — path traversal or invalid input.

get.description: Return a snapshot of the in-flight and completed task counters.

Return export entries where the expected output file is missing or has
zero bytes on disk — indicating the photo needs to be re-processed.

schema.title: Response List Conflicts Api V1 Projects  Project Id  Exports Conflicts Get

post.description:
Apply *body.action* to the matching conflict records.

- ``action="replace"`` resets state to ``new`` (re-queues for processing).
- ``action="skip"``    marks the photo as ``skip``.

200.description: Number of resolved conflicts and the action applied.

schema.title: Response Resolve Conflicts Api V1 Projects  Project Id  Exports Conflicts Resolve Post

get.description: Return a summary record for every project directory in PROJECTS_DIR.

schema.title: Response List Projects Api V1 Projects Get

post.description:
Create a new project directory with *settings.json* and *export_option.json*.

The project identifier (URL segment) is derived by replacing spaces with
underscores in *body.name*.  Returns **409 Conflict** if a project with the
same normalised name already exists.

get.description: Return full details for a single project.

delete.description:
Permanently remove the project directory and all its files from disk.

**This action is irreversible.**  Source and export files are NOT deleted —
only the project metadata directory is removed.

get.description: Return the contents of the project's *settings.json*.

put.description: Overwrite the project's *settings.json* with *body* and return the saved values.

get.description: Return all export profiles defined in the project's *export_option.json*.

put.description: Replace the project's *export_option.json* with the supplied profiles dict.

post.description:
Run the BID validator against *body.profile* and return a list of any errors.
The project directory does not need any existing profiles for this to work.

get.description:
Return the immutable audit trail for a project, newest first.

Filterable by `action` (e.g. `state_change`, `metadata_update`) and `folder`.
Supports pagination via `limit` / `offset`.

schema.title: Response Get Audit Logs Api V1 Projects  Project Id  Audit Logs Get

AuditLogEntry.title: AuditLogEntry
AuditLogEntry.description: Single audit log record returned by GET /audit/logs.

ConflictItem.title: ConflictItem
ConflictItem.description: A single blocked / missing export entry.

ConflictResolutionRequest.title: ConflictResolutionRequest
ConflictResolutionRequest.description:
Request body for POST /exports/conflicts/resolve.

mode:
  "all"       – apply action to every conflict in the project.
  "selection" – apply action to the photos listed in `items`.

action:
  "replace"  – reset state to "new" so the photo is re-queued.
  "skip"     – mark the photo as "skip" (do not process again).

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description: Standard error envelope returned by exception handlers.

ExportProfileValidateResponse.title: ExportProfileValidateResponse
ExportProfileValidateResponse.description: Validation result for a single export profile.

ExportProfilesResponse.title: ExportProfilesResponse
ExportProfilesResponse.description: Response for GET /export-profiles — maps profile name → profile definition.

PhotoTaskStatus.title: PhotoTaskStatus
PhotoTaskStatus.description: Status snapshot of a single in-flight processing task.

photos.title: Photos
photos.description: List of (folder, photo) pairs to process.

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.description: Export profile keys to apply.  None = all profiles.

ProcessRequest.title: ProcessRequest
ProcessRequest.description:
Body for POST /projects/{id}/process.

photos: list of (folder, photo) tuples identifying the source photos to process.
profiles: optional allowlist of export profile keys.  None means all profiles.

ProcessResponse.title: ProcessResponse
ProcessResponse.description: Returned immediately after photos are enqueued (non-blocking).

ProcessStatusResponse.title: ProcessStatusResponse
ProcessStatusResponse.description: Current state of the processing queue for a project.

name.title: Name
name.description: Project display name (only letters, digits, spaces, underscores, hyphens).

source_folder.title: Source Folder
source_folder.description: Absolute path to the source folder.

export_folder.title: Export Folder
export_folder.description: Absolute path to the export folder.

ProjectInfo.title: ProjectInfo
ProjectInfo.description: Summary record for one project — returned in list and detail responses.

source_folder.title: Source Folder
source_folder.description: Absolute path to the source image folder.

export_folder.title: Export Folder
export_folder.description: Absolute path to the export destination.

failed_total.title: Failed Total
failed_total.description: Tasks that ended in error since last restart.

QueueMetricsResponse.title: QueueMetricsResponse
QueueMetricsResponse.description: Operational metrics for the processing queue.

UserResponse.title: UserResponse
UserResponse.description: Public representation of a user record (no password).

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description: Partial update — only provided fields are changed.

tags.name: system
tags.description: Liveness and version probes, and queue-level operational metrics.  These endpoints require no authentication and are intended for load-balancer health checks and monitoring dashboards.

tags.name: processing
tags.description: Image-processing pipeline control for a single project.  Enqueue selected or all *new* photos, reset photos to *new* state, and query the real-time processing-queue snapshot.  Processing is non-blocking: the enqueue call returns immediately while work proceeds in background threads.

tags.name: exports
tags.description: Export-conflict management.  Lists photos whose expected output file is missing or zero-byte (*blocked exports*) and provides a bulk-resolve action to either re-queue (`replace`) or permanently skip (`skip`) conflicting entries.

tags.name: projects
tags.description: Project directory CRUD, settings management, and export-profile configuration.  Projects are on-disk directories under `PROJECTS_DIR`; each contains `settings.json` (source/export folder paths) and `export_option.json` (profile definitions).  Also exposes the immutable per-project audit-log stream.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: JWT-based authentication.  POST `/auth/login` with username + password to receive an access token (30 min, default) and a refresh token (7 days, default).  Pass the access token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

tags.name: users
tags.description: User account management — **admin role required**.  Supports create, list, update (role/state/email), and delete operations.

tags.name: websocket
tags.description: WebSocket real-time event streaming (Phase 2).  Connect to `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/ws?token=<access_token>` to receive `state_change`, `progress`, `scan_update`, `queue_metrics`, and `error` frames as processing progresses.  Send `{"type":"subscribe","folders":[...]}` to filter by sub-folder.  The server sends periodic `ping` frames; reply with `pong` to keep the connection alive.
